methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.3B - $5.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Form Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $3.5B

data.narrative:
TAE Technologies, a Series G climate and energy company founded by Rostoker and Binderbauer, commands a current post-money valuation of $6.0B following $1.3B+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-or-adjacent-stage comparables (Helion at $5.4B Series F, Form Energy at $3.5B Series G, Sila Nanotechnologies at $3.3B late stage) alongside 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage. Stage-progression methodology, anchoring to the $6.0B last post-money and applying a typical 1.25x Series G step-up multiplier, implies $7.5B. Peer-median analysis yields $3.5B, reflecting the median across the 3-company comparable set with a range of $3.3B to $5.4B. The divergence between methods reflects both TAE's larger fundraise and the spread across the peer cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $3.0B to $8.6B, with a median cross-sectional value of $5.5B. The current $6.0B post-money sits above the peer median but below the stage-progression upside, suggesting the valuation reflects company-specific factors relative to peers. Key uncertainties include the maturity and capital efficiency profiles of comparable companies and whether TAE's subsequent-round pricing will move toward peer multiples or sustain step-up momentum.
